observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management:

direct the Municipal Planning and Development Office (MPDO) alongside with GAD Focal Point System to work on the establishment of updated GAD database to ensure an effective and efficient implementation of GAD; and


ensure that all GAD planned PPAs are fully implemented so that government’s commitment to women’s empowerment and gender equality in their locality is attained.
observations.managementAction:
Establishment of GAD database for funding in the CY 2021 Annual Budget.



Implementation of GAD PPAs for CY 2020.
observations.reason: The CY 2021 Annual Budget of the LGU is still pending approval.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit immediately the subject/needed lacking supporting documents to facilitate technical review and evaluation of the completed projects; and refrain from approving government claims with incomplete supporting documents.
observations.reason: The subject lacking documents were not yet submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:
record the liquidation of petty operating expenses amounting to ₱107,800.00; and
discontinue using PCF to facilitate cash payment to AICS beneficiaries and grant cash advances to designated Special Disbursing Officer for this particular program.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:
prepare the supporting schedules such as the Status of Unliquidated Cash Advances for all Advances (1-03-05) account, monthly Schedule of Aging of Receivables and Subsidiary Ledger of cash advances per employee to support the balance of the outstanding cash advances in the financial statement and consequently enforce the liquidation/settlement thereof;
use the abovementioned proposed journal entries from the funding up to the distribution of salaries and wages of employees in order to reflect the correct nature of transaction in adherence to the instructions under COA Circular No. 2015-009; and
Management committed to comply with the audit recommendations.

We recommended that Management:
require the Municipal Treasurer to promptly submit the list of taxpayers and the computed amount due and collectible for the year to correctly establish the receivable taxes for each year; and
direct the Municipal Accountant to adjust the RPT/SET Receivables accounts and regularly record the correct tax receivables based on the established collectibles for the year to reflect a realistic collectible taxes each year.

We recommended that Management:
allocate budget for the registration of the Land recognized in the financial statements; and
initiate the processes in securing the transfer certificate of titles (TCT) on the purchased lots to support claims of ownership to fully protect the interest of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management:
create an Inventory Committee who shall be responsible for the actual count to ascertain the existence, completeness and condition of all PPEs owned by the agency and prepare a Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) and follow the specific guidelines and procedures as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-006 accordingly;
reconcile the current balances of PPE accounts in the Statement of Financial Position as against the general ledger of PPE, the PPE ledger cards against the general ledger and the PPELCs against the RPCPPE to support the balances of PPE in the Statement of Financial Position; and
consistently update the PPELCs and RPCPPEs and revise the same using the prescribed formats.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:
cause for the immediate remittance of the past due taxes withheld;
maintain and consistently update the subsidiary ledger for Due to BIR (202-01-020) account to properly reflect the balance for monthly remittance; and
facilitate the monthly remittance in full and within the prescribed period of all taxes to be withheld to avoid possible penalties.

We recommended that Management:
closely coordinate with the source agencies and exert efforts in retrieving the supporting documents for the ₱1,421,781.83 funds which were already disbursed to expedite their liquidation and thereafter be recorded in the books of the LGU;
facilitate the return of unused funds for completed projects amounting to ₱463,112.38 to the BTR this CY 2021; and
adhere strictly with the provision of COA Circular No. 94-013 on the liquidation of fund transfers once projects are fully implemented and/or refund the unutilized amounts from completed projects and subsequently require for the appropriate recording in the books of accounts.

We recommended that Management inquire and clarify with the GPPB the possibility of belated posting in the GPPB online portal of purchases through emergency procurement during CY 2020.  Likewise, prospectively ensure that procurement activities are posted in the GPPB online portal as required by law.
We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the guidelines and procedures of release and utilization of the grants it will receive in the future.

We recommended that Management comply and submit the aforementioned lacking documents on the noted DRRM Fund transactions amounting to ₱6.18 million for proper audit and verification and to avoid audit suspension or disallowance.

We recommended that Management create a well-planned LDF program to minimize changes that cause unnecessary delays and prepare catch up plan, if necessary, to promptly deliver the expected benefits of the LDF projects to the intended beneficiaries.

We recommended that Management:
consider designating additional manpower in the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and Business Permits and Licensing Office to aid in the collection of the various taxes and fees being collected by the Municipality; and
direct the MTO and BPLO to adopt measures and work hand in hand in intensifying tax collections efforts in order to maximize the collections of the Municipality and meet its projected revenue to fund its development projects.

We recommend that Management create a well-planned LDF program to minimize changes that cause unnecessary delays and prepare catch up plan, if necessary, to promptly deliver the expected benefits of the LDF projects to the intended beneficiaries; and see to it that projects are implemented within the budget year.
observations.managementAction: Compliance with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:

prepare the necessary accounting adjusting journal entries in order to recognize the aforementioned infrastructure projects in the books;


record accumulated costs of infrastructure projects to construction in progress accounts or in the appropriate infrastructure assets upon completion; and 

maintain required schedule/list and subsidiary ledgers as well as update PPE ledger cards to properly account the ongoing and completed infrastructure assets of the LGU.
observations.managementAction:
The subject infrastructure projects were already completed and recorded in the appropriate asset accounts. 

Identify on-going and completed projects and prepare necessary adjusting entries to book up cost of projects. 
The Accounting Office maintained the required schedules and conducts monthly reconciliation of the SL and GL balances.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:

prepare the necessary accounting adjusting journal entries in order to recognize the aforementioned acquired properties in the books;

ensure that all cost of acquired properties are recorded to their appropriate asset accounts; and 

maintain required schedule/list and subsidiary ledgers as well as update PPE ledger cards to properly account the acquired properties of the LGU.
observations.managementAction:
Recording of necessary adjusting entries in the books. 


Identified cost of acquired properties and prepare necessary journal entries to book up the same. 
Compliance with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management:

require the concerned officers and employees to immediately settle/liquidate their outstanding cash advances through the submission of duly accomplished liquidation reports, otherwise, enforce the withholding of salary, in accordance with Section 5.1.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002; 

stop the grant of additional cash advances for travel and training to all personnel with unliquidated cash advances until such time previous cash advances are fully settled; and
monitor and establish controls to ensure the timely liquidation of cash advances through updated monthly aging of the same to prevent accumulation of unliquidated cash advances.
observations.managementAction:
Compliance with the audit recommendation.


There is still an on-going reconciliation of the cash advance accounts. 

The target date for completion of the reconciliation is within the 1st quarter of CY 2021.
observations.reason:
The balance of unliquidated cash advances was reduced to ₱3,025,773.26 as of December 31, 2020.

This is restated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere to Section 3.0 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 and record transferred funds/donations as financial assistance with specific purpose to appropriate receivable accounts and require the recipients to liquidate funds received, for proper monitoring of the utilization thereof to avoid unwarranted loss/misuse of government funds.
observations.managementAction: Strict adherence to COA Circular No. 2012-001 on the accounting and recording of financial assistance granted by LGUs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management:

direct the Accountant, GSO and Requisitioning Party/Office to follow the required flow of transactions and accomplish/update records/reports in processing purchase and requisition of supplies and materials for proper monitoring, recording and accounting of delivered and issued, consumed/distributed inventories; and
record supplies and materials deemed inventory assets as inventories first and their corresponding expense accounts once issued/consumed/distributed.
observations.managementAction:
The Accounting Office maintains the necessary Subsidiary Ledgers for inventory accounts. 


Adherence to the provisions of PPSAS No. 12 on Inventories.
observations.reason: Restated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:

adhere with the required accounting and recording of PPE and assign an employee who shall prepare the supporting schedules and reports on a full-time basis, for reliable presentation and reporting of PPE accounts; and

assign an employee to coordinate with the GSO in the reconciliation of the recorded PPE with the latter’s RPCPPE and Property Cards, also on a full-time basis.
observations.managementAction:
Adherence to the required accounting and recording of PPE. Two employees were assigned to maintain records and schedules of PPEs. 

Reconciliation of the two records is still on-going.
observations.reason: Discrepancies were noted between the balance of PPE per books and RPCPPE submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to effect adjusting entries to correct the erroneous transactions recorded in the Cash-Local Treasury account and to bring the account to its correct balance in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The accounting office is still verifying its records to identify necessary adjusting entries to be made.
observations.reason: Retrieval of old files manually takes a lot of time.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management direct the:

Local Finance Committee to conduct a thorough review of the Municipality’s resources and budget;

Municipal Treasurer to adopt efficient, effective and economic measures to intensify revenue collection efforts and improve the LGU’s collection system and revenue generation; and

Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Budget Officer in updating the budget in accordance with the status of financial operations and closely monitor the expenses to avoid incurrence of loss/deficit and strictly adhere to Section 337 of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: Compliance with the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: This is discussed in Part II of this Report.

observations.managementAction:
Adoption of imprest system in the payment of financial assistance. 

Compliance with the audit recommendation. 

Compliance with the audit recommendation.

Compliance with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management:

direct the Municipal Treasurer to conduct and intensify tax campaigns to maximize collections especially on the delinquent taxpayers and impose remedies provided for, and exercise the taxing authority as mandated under the Local Government Code; and 
seek the assistance of the respective barangay officials in the delivery of notices of delinquency and subsequent collection thereof.
observations.managementAction:
The MTO conducted regular tax campaigns and massive information dissemination to barangays. 

Compliance with the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: Management cannot fully conduct face to face collections and tax collection campaigns due to the pandemic.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:

maintain SL for payable accounts and regularly update posting of transactions in the books at all times and reconcile the same with the General Ledger (GL) balance;

review the composition of Accounts Payable through verification of documents and reports related to prior years’ transactions in order to identify valid claims/obligations; and

regularly prepare and maintain a schedule of aging of payable accounts and cause for the reversion to the unappropriated surplus of the identified payables without valid claimants, in compliance with applicable rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction:
Preparation of SL for payable accounts and reconciliation of SL and GL balances.

Verification of records is still on-going. 

Compliance with the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: Difficulty in tracing documents related to transactions of prior years.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management direct the:

Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the previous Municipal Accountant to gather all the details and information needed to review the infrastructure assets prior to the adoption of PPSAS to ascertain the correctness and completeness of the beginning balances of all the LGUs infrastructure assets;

Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary subsidiary ledgers, lapsing schedules, complete PPELC and disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements to support the balance of the Road Network System (1-07-03-010) account; and

General Services Officer to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer in conducting the inventory of all local roads and strictly comply with the reports required under COA Circular No. 2015-008.
observations.managementAction:
Assigned one employee to handle verification of previous records. 


Assigned one employee to work on preparation of schedules and inventory report on Road Networks.


Conduct of inventory of the LGU’s local roads.
observations.reason:
Gathering of data and previous records is still on-going.


There were no lapsing schedules submitted and appropriate disclosure of Road Networks in the financial statements. 


The inventory of road networks was included in the line item "Land" on the submitted RPCPPE as of December 31, 2020 and not as a separate PPE account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the state policy on DRRM by giving priority on the approval and implementation of planned LDRRM PPAs and create well-planned activities for all PPAs of the LGU to avoid too much delay in its implementation.
observations.managementAction: Hiring of additional MDRRMO personnel to assist in the implementation of PPAs funded under the LDRRMF.

Finalization of the 10-year SWM Plan of the Municipality of Calabanga.
The following are some of  the SWM PPAs implemented by the LGU:

Organization and Capacitation of the Information and Education Campaign (IEC);

Establishment of Barangay Materials Recovery Facility (MRF);

Conduct of Ecological Solid Waste Management Orientation to 23 barangays; and 

Organization of Municipal Ecological Solid Waste Management Board composed of 18 multi-sectoral representatives.
Enactment of Ordinance No. 2017-03 and implementation of such as well as ordinances on “Plastice-to-Rice Swap” and “Walang Plastikan” aimed at minimizing generation of plastic waste. 

Compliance with the audit recommendation.


Implementation of Closure and Rehabilitation Plan at Bigaas Dumpsite.

Construction of Residual Containment Area (RCA) and MRF at Bigaas Dumpsite.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:

direct the Municipal Assessor to closely coordinate with the Provincial Assessor of the Province of Camarines Sur to raise the issue of general revision of assessment of real properties and passage of new SFMV with fully documented course of action on this matter; 

require the Municipal Assessor to conduct periodic ocular inspection of real properties within the LGU’s jurisdiction to appraise and assess newly constructed and occupied properties as well as the taxable improvements in order to update tax levied and collected from taxpayers; and

employ other collection strategies such as having additional barangay treasurers as deputized collecting officers for the LGU and giving performance incentives to revenue collection clerks of the LGU to enforce collection of unpaid taxes, if necessary.
observations.managementAction:
Coordinated with the Provincial Assessor for the passage of new SFMV. 


Conduct of ocular properties on some of the real properties within the LGU. 


Management sought the help of the barangays in the enforcement and collection of delinquent taxes.
observations.reason:
Revision of SMFV is on-going. Management shall create an inspection team to ensure that the 48 barangays will be covered in the revision. 

Lack of manpower who will conduct periodic and complete ocular inspection of real properties within the LGU. 

The ordinance on having Barangay Treasurers as deputized collecting officers of the LGU is still pending approval by the Sangguniang Bayan.

observations.recommendation: conduct periodic reconciliation of the reciprocal accounts in order to identify possible errors/adjustments which need to be recorded in the books.
observations.managementAction: Verification and reconciliation of entries made for previous years’ transactions is still on-going.
observations.reason: The unreconciled difference between the reciprocal accounts decreased to ₱883,415.93 as of December 31, 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to exert all necessary efforts to finish the reconciliation of the Due to BIR balances among the different accounts of the LGU so that any resulting reconciling items will be promptly given appropriate action.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation of the balance of the Due to BIR account.
observations.reason:
Difficulty in retrieving records of prior years’ transactions.

This is restated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to hasten the reconciliation and prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct balance of the trust fund accounts.

observations.reason: The balance of transferred funds from NGAs under the General Fund amounted to ₱(836,158.06) as of December 31, 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require strict adherence to the aforementioned COA Circular on the timely submission of Report of Cash Disbursements and/or Liquidation Reports for all settled cash advances to allow proper verification by the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: Timely submission of Cash Disbursement and Liquidation Reports.
observations.reason: Reconciliation of accounting records and MTO’s records is still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to exert efforts in retrieving the disbursement vouchers and corresponding supporting documents of the noted unrecorded items and to effect necessary adjustments in the books to bring the Cash in Bank-LCCA to its correct balance in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Recording of valid reconciling items in the books of the LGU.
observations.reason: There were noted reconciling items in the Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS) as of December 31, 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant and GSO to adhere with the required accounting and recording of inventory and allot time to reconcile each other’s records for reliable presentation and reporting of inventory accounts; and direct the GSO to prepare, submit and update the proper form and content of Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) and Stock Cards and ensure that issuance of supplies is properly documented and transmitted to accounting for recording.
observations.managementAction: The accounting office maintained Subsidiary Schedules (SL) for the LGU’s inventory accounts as of December 31, 2020.
observations.reason: This is discussed in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to effect adjusting entries to correct the erroneous transactions recorded in the Cash-Local Treasury account and to bring the account to its correct balance in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: Restated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require strict adherence to the aforementioned COA Circular on the timely submission of Report of Cash Disbursements and/or Liquidation Reports for all settled cash advances to allow proper verification by the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: Coordination with the concerned accountable officer and strict adherence to applicable COA rules and regulations on submission of Liquidation Report.
observations.reason: Transmittal of some liquidation reports to the accounting office by designated disbursing officers is 1-2 months delayed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to effect adjusting entries to correct the erroneous transactions recorded in the Cash-Local Treasury account and to bring the account to its correct balance in the financial statements.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
require the concerned officers and employees to immediately settle/liquidate their outstanding cash advances through the submission of duly accomplished liquidation reports, otherwise, enforce the withholding of salary, in accordance with Section 5.1.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002;
stop the grant of additional cash advances for travel and training to all personnel with unliquidated cash advances until such time previous cash advances are fully settled; and
monitor and establish controls to ensure the timely liquidation of cash advances through updated monthly aging of the same to prevent accumulation of unliquidated cash advances.

We recommended that Management:
direct the Accountant, GSO and Requisitioning Party/Office to follow the required flow of transactions and accomplish/update records/reports in processing purchase and requisition of supplies and materials for proper monitoring, recording and accounting of delivered and issued/consumed/distributed inventories; and
record supplies and materials deemed inventory assets as inventories first and their corresponding expense accounts once issued/consumed/distributed.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:
prepare the necessary accounting adjusting journal entries in order to recognize the aforementioned acquired properties in the books;
ensure that all cost of acquired properties are recorded to their appropriate asset accounts; and
maintain required schedule/list and subsidiary ledgers as well as update PPE ledger cards to properly account the acquired properties of the LGU.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:
adhere with the required accounting and recording of PPE and assign an employee who shall prepare the supporting schedules and reports on a full-time basis, for reliable presentation and reporting of PPE accounts; and
assign an employee to coordinate with the GSO in the reconciliation of the recorded PPE with the latter’s RPCPPE and Property Cards, also on a full-time basis.

We recommended that Management direct the:
Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the previous Municipal Accountant to gather all the details and information needed to review the infrastructure assets prior to the adoption of PPSAS to ascertain the correctness and completeness of the beginning balances of all the LGUs infrastructure assets;
Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary subsidiary ledgers, lapsing schedules, complete PPELC and disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements to support the balance of the Road Network System (1-07-03-010) account; and
General Services Officer to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer in conducting the inventory of all local roads and strictly comply with the reports required under COA Circular No. 2015-008.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
prepare the necessary accounting adjusting journal entries in order to recognize the aforementioned infrastructure projects in the books;
record accumulated costs of infrastructure projects to construction in progress accounts or in the appropriate infrastructure assets upon completion; and
maintain required schedule/list and subsidiary ledgers as well as update PPE ledger cards to properly account the ongoing and completed infrastructure assets of the LGU.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:
maintain Subsidiary Ledgers (SL) for payable accounts and regularly update posting of transactions in the books at all times and reconcile the same with the General Ledger (GL) balance;
review the composition of Accounts Payable through verification of documents and reports related to prior years’ transactions in order to identify valid claims/obligations; and
regularly prepare and maintain a schedule of aging of payable accounts and cause for the reversion to the unappropriated surplus of the identified payables without valid claimants, in compliance with applicable rules and regulations.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to: (a) strictly adhere to Section 3.0 of COA Circular No. 2012-001; and (b) record transferred funds/donations as financial assistance with specific purpose to appropriate receivable accounts and require the recipients to liquidate funds received, for proper monitoring of the utilization thereof to avoid unwarranted loss/misuse of government funds.

We recommended that Management: (a) create a well-planned LDF program to minimize changes that cause unnecessary delays and prepare catch up plan, if necessary, to promptly deliver the expected benefits of the LDF projects to the intended beneficiaries; and (b) see to it that projects are implemented within the budget year.

We recommended that Management:
direct the Municipal Planning and Development Office (MPDO) alongside with GAD Focal Point System to work on the establishment of updated GAD database to ensure an effective and efficient implementation of GAD; and
ensure that all GAD planned PPAs are fully implemented so that government’s commitment to women’s empowerment and gender equality in their locality is attained.

We recommended that Management:
direct the Municipal Treasurer to conduct and intensify tax campaigns to maximize collections especially on the delinquent taxpayers and impose remedies provided for, and exercise the taxing authority as mandated under the Local Government Code; and
seek the assistance of the respective barangay officials in the delivery of notices of delinquency and subsequent collection thereof.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the:
Local Finance Committee to conduct a thorough review of the Municipality’s resources and budget;
Municipal Treasurer to adopt efficient, effective and economic measures to intensify revenue collection efforts and improve the LGU’s collection system and revenue generation; and
Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Budget Officer in updating the budget in accordance with the status of financial operations and closely monitor the expenses to avoid incurrence of loss/deficit and strictly adhere to Section 337 of RA 7160.

We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the state policy on DRRM by giving priority on the approval and implementation of planned LDRRM PPAs and create well-planned activities for all PPAs of the LGU to avoid too much delay in its implementation.
observations.reason:
Some of the CY 2019 projects funded under the 70 per cent Mitigation Fund such as the procurement of communication equipment for Information and Education Campaign and stockpiling of goods were not fully completed due to time constraints in their implementation.

The total unimplemented PPAs under the 70% Mitigation Fund amounted to ₱2,890,000.00 as of December 31, 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: exert effort to facilitate the approval of the LGU’s SWM Plan for proper, clearer, timely and systematic implementation of SWM; implement and closely monitor significant SWM PPAs as planned and budgeted to maximize the benefits that could be derived therefrom; willfully enforce existing ordinances and their penalties and/or incentives and/or add SWM ordinances, if necessary, to effectively implement SWM; adopt additional specific revenue-generating measures to promote the viability of the LGU’s Solid Waste Management Plan; and hasten the completion of the LGU’s Materials Recovery Facility (MRF) and Residual Containment Area (RCA) as well as complete closure of the existing dumpsite according to the set timeline.
observations.reason:
The following additional structures for the proper implementation of SWM (funds realigned from 20 per cent EDF) were reported to be on-going as of year-end:

Putting up of Temporary Residual Containment Area (RCA)

Fence Completion

Canal and Leading Chambers

Earthworks

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly adhere with GAD policies to implement GAD related activities and designate person responsible that will handle/focus only with GAD PPAs, if necessary, to be able to implement all GAD PPAs as planned/budgeted to achieve GAD’s objectives and address gender issues.
observations.managementAction: Implementation of GAD PPAs for CY 2018.
observations.reason:
The Municipality’s designated GAD Focal Person resigned on October 2018 and as of December 2019, Management has still not assigned a new GAD Focal Person.

Restated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:

direct the Municipal Assessor to closely coordinate with the Provincial Assessor of the Province of Camarines Sur to raise the issue of general revision of assessment of real properties and passage of new SFMV with fully documented course of action on this matter; 


require the Municipal Assessor to conduct periodic ocular inspection of real properties within the LGU’s jurisdiction to appraise and assess newly constructed and occupied properties as well as the taxable improvements in order to update tax levied and collected from taxpayers; and

employ other collection strategies such as having additional barangay treasurers as deputized collecting officers for the LGU and giving performance incentives to revenue collection clerks of the LGU to enforce collection of unpaid taxes, if necessary.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Assessor shall coordinate with the Provincial Assessor with regard to the request for the revision of the assessment of real properties and updating of the SFMV.

Review, assessment and reorganization plan has been drafted under the new leadership and submitted to the Local Chief Executive.

Proposal to deputize Barangay Treasurers as RPT Collectors has been submitted to the Sangguniang Bayan for review and study.
observations.reason:
There is still no general revision of assessment real properties and updating of the SFMV from the Province of Camarines Sur.


Delayed implementation due to change in leadership within the office.


Data encoding at the Assessor’s Office is still on-going.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:

employ necessary action to speed up the encoding of the accounting transactions to the LGU’s accounting system to expedite the submission of the financial reports to COA for proper review and verification; and

review/assess the economy, effectiveness and efficiency of the LGU’s computerized accounting system and take necessary actions to improve it so that subsequent reports are submitted on time.
observations.managementAction: Compliance with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

exert efforts in reconciling the balances of reciprocal accounts and maintain SLs for receivable and payable accounts as well as other assets and expense accounts and regularly reconcile the amount with GL balances and update recording/posting of transactions at all times; and

conduct periodic reconciliation of the reciprocal accounts in order to identify possible errors/adjustments which need to be recorded in the books.
observations.managementAction:
Assigned one employee to prioritize retrieval of and verification from previous years’ records and another employee to maintain subsidiary ledgers.

Reconciliation of the reciprocal accounts is still on-going.
observations.reason: Unavailability of previous years’ records.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

instruct all concerned municipal officials to monitor the implementation of the unimplemented and on-going projects year to year to facilitate the prompt completion thereof to immediately deliver the expected benefits to the public; and 

create a well-planned program of implementation of 20 per cent LDF PPAs which includes timelines, mode of procurement and other relevant procurement steps that usually affect the timeliness of project completion so that delays in the implementation of LDF and other projects are avoided.
observations.managementAction: Compliance with the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: Restated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management coordinate with the source agencies of the fund and return the balance and/or request for authority to use the remaining fund for related programs or projects beneficial to the interest of the constituents.
observations.managementAction: Compliance with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere to Section 69 of PD 1445 and remit in full and within the prescribed period all taxes withheld from individuals and LGU’s supplier; and

exert all necessary efforts to finish the reconciliation of the Due to BIR balances among the different accounts of the LGU so that any resulting reconciling items will be promptly given appropriate action.
observations.managementAction:
Management observed timely remittance of withheld taxes during the year.

Assigned one employee to prioritize retrieval of and verification of prior years’ records.
observations.reason:
Retrieval of previous years’ documents and reconciliation of the balances is still on-going.

The amount of unremitted withheld taxes was reduced to ₱1,981,351.47 as of December 31, 2019.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the program developer and oblige them to modify or correct the computer program that will suit to the needs of the accounting of the LGU and to simplify the use of the system and ensure that the outputs generated by the system would comply with the those required by the e-NGAS of the Commission on Audit for accurate and reliable data as well as for proper and timely verification of the LGU’s account; closely monitor accounting staff or systems users to identify/determine issues and concern related thereto for proper and appropriate action; and
make representation with COA Central Office relative to the installation of e-NGAS in the municipality.
observations.managementAction: Compliance with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management adhere strictly with Section 3 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 and Section 4(6) of PD 1445 at all times and submit and attach complete contract documents and required supporting documents to government disbursements; submit immediately the subject/needed lacking supporting documents to facilitate technical review and evaluation of the completed projects; and refrain from approving government claims with incomplete supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: Submission (in partial) of the lacking documents for review and verification by the Audit Team, namely: Pre-Bid Conference Notice and Proof of Posting to PhilGEPS of Notice to Proceed.
observations.reason: There are still lacking documents for transmittal to the Audit Team, such as: Bid Evaluation Report and Post-Qualification Notice to Observers.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:

utilize the LGU’s approved counterpart fund on the subject projects with overspent trust funds;

plan and execute well the project implementation of  the planned program, projects and activities (PPAs) under the trust fund such as securing the budget/fund and  the LGU’s counterpart fund and other necessary plan of actions before implementing PPAs to avoid overspending the funds available for a particular special project and keep the funds of other projects intact; and

hasten the reconciliation and prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct balance of the trust fund accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to effect adjusting entries to correct the erroneous transactions recorded in the Cash-Local Treasury account and to bring the account to its correct balance in the financial statements.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to exert efforts in retrieving the disbursement vouchers and corresponding supporting documents of the noted unrecorded items and to effect necessary adjustments in the books to bring the Cash in Bank-LCCA to its correct balance in the financial statements.

We recommended and Management agreed to require strict adherence to the aforementioned COA Circular on the timely submission of Report of Cash Disbursements/Liquidation Reports for all settled cash advances to allow proper verification by the Audit Team.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
maintain close coordination within the accounting office and exert efforts in reconciling the balances of reciprocal accounts and maintain SLs for receivable and payable accounts as well as other assets and expense accounts and regularly reconcile the amount with GL balances and update recording/posting of transactions at all times; and
conduct periodic reconciliation of the reciprocal accounts in order to identify possible errors/adjustments which need to be recorded in the books.

We recommended that Management:
direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere to Section 69 of PD 1445 and remit in full and within the prescribed period all taxes withheld from individuals and LGU’s supplier; and
exert all necessary efforts to finish the reconciliation of the Due to BIR balances among the different accounts of the LGU so that any resulting reconciling items will be promptly given appropriate action.

We recommended that Management coordinate with the source agencies of the fund and return the balance and/or request for authority to use the remaining fund for related programs or projects beneficial to the interest of the constituents.

We recommended that Management:
instruct all concerned municipal officials to monitor the implementation of the unimplemented and on-going projects year to year to facilitate the prompt completion thereof to immediately deliver the expected benefits of the LDF projects to the public; and
create a well-planned program of implementation of 20 per cent LDF PPAs as well as other PPAs under other funds that which includes timelines, mode of procurement and other relevant procurement steps that usually affect the timeliness of project completion so that delays in the implementation of LDF and other projects are avoided.

We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the state policy on DRRM by giving priority on the approval and implementation of planned LDRRM PPAs and create well-planned activities for all PPAs of the LGU to avoid too much delay in its implementation.

We recommended that Management strictly adhere with GAD policies to implement GAD related activities and designate person responsible that will handle/focus only with GAD PPAs, if necessary, to be able to implement all GAD PPAs as planned/budgeted to achieve GAD’s objectives and address gender issues.

We recommended that the Management:
utilize the LGU’s approved counterpart fund on the subject projects with overspent trust funds;
plan and execute well the project implementation of the planned program, projects and activities (PPAs) under the trust fund such as securing the budget/fund and the LGU’s counterpart fund and other necessary plan of actions before implementing PPAs to avoid overspending the funds available for a particular special project and keep the funds of other projects intact; and
hasten the reconciliation and prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct balance of the trust fund accounts.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
coordinate with the program developer and oblige them to modify or correct the computer program that will suit to the needs of the accounting of the LGU and to simplify the use of the system and ensure that the outputs generated by the system would comply with the those required by the e-NGAS of the Commission on Audit for accurate and reliable data as well as for proper and timely verification of the LGU’s account;
direct the Municipal Accountant to closely monitor accounting staff or systems users to identify/determine issues and concern related thereto for proper and appropriate action; and
make representation with COA Central Office relative to the installation of e-NGAS in the municipality.

We recommended that the Management:
direct the Municipal Assessor to closely coordinate with the Provincial Assessor of the Province of Camarines Sur to raise the issue of general revision of assessment of real properties and passage of new SFMV with fully documented course of action on this matter;
require the Municipal Assessor to conduct periodic ocular inspection of real properties within the LGU’s jurisdiction to appraise and assess newly constructed and occupied properties by virtue of Section 204 “Declaration of Real Property by the Assessor” of RA 7160 as well as the taxable improvements to properties in order to update tax levied and collected from taxpayers; and
employ other collection strategies such as having additional barangay treasurers as deputized collecting officers for the LGU and giving performance incentives to revenue collection clerks of the LGU to enforce collection of unpaid taxes, if necessary.

Management is advised to:

require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that adjustments be made in the books of the municipality to recognize the unrecorded valid check disbursements and present the actual balance of the Cash and other related accounts;

require the Municipal Treasurer to transmit paid disbursement vouchers to the Accounting Office; and

transmit to COA the afore-mentioned unsubmitted disbursement vouchers and BRS as required by rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Recording of the identified unrecorded disbursements for CY 2017, the list of which are submitted to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: There are still unrecorded disbursements for CY 2018 and previous years, per the latest bank reconciliation statements.

Management is advised to:

direct the Municipal Accountant to 1) initiate efforts to be able to prepare and submit the Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS) on a monthly basis; and 2) prepare immediately the required BRS and adjust the identified reconciling items accordingly; and 

instruct the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that deposits and charges/withdrawals are made to the correct bank accounts to avoid erroneous records and expedite transmittal of reports to the accounting office to facilitate timely recording of transactions.

observations.reason: Delayed issuance of bank statements of depository banks.

observations.recommendation:
It is recommended that Management:

pre-terminate the time deposit account totaling ₱18,514,408.50 and transfer back the account to its current account; 

investigate and determine the details and specific purpose of the said fund placed in time deposit account and immediately implement the same; and

in case/s of unexpended balance and savings from implemented projects from Trust Fund, coordinate with the source agencies (SA) of the fund and return the balance, if the SA is no longer in the service/or cannot be determined, deposit the amount directly to the Bureau of the Treasury.

observations.managementAction: Close monitoring of the implementation of projects, programs and activities funder under the 20% Local Development Fund.

observations.recommendation: We reiterate our previous year’s audit recommendations that Management: (a) Require the concerned officers and employees to immediately settle/liquidate their outstanding cash advances thru the submission of duly accomplished liquidation reports; otherwise withhold any money due them; (b) Stop the grant of additional cash advances for travel to all personnel with unliquidated cash advance until such time these are fully settled; and (c) Monitor and establish controls to ensure the timely submission of liquidation documents and full accounting of balances of cash advance by enforcing strictly the prescribed period of liquidation.

observations.reason: Restated in Part II of this Report. Unliquidated Cash Advances increased to P1,576,921.24 in CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management instruct all concerned officials for coordination with concerned offices and institute improvements in the procurement process in the soonest possible time to achieve efficient delivery of goods and services; and (b) Instruct the GSO to revisit the procurement rules taking into consideration the timelines set by RA 9184.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management: (a) Evaluate the Revised IRR of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 (a) Evaluate the Revised IRR of RA 9184.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management immediately reconcile the General Ledger and Subsidiary Ledger balances in order to account the difference of P6,199,118.18 and maintain Subsidiary Ledger for General Fund.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 Reconciliation of SL and GL of Due to NGAs account under Trust Fund.
observations.reason: For CY 2017, no Subsidiary Ledger (SL) was maintained for the Due to NGAs account under the General Fund.

observations.recommendation: Instruct the OIC Municipal Treasurer/Acting General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant to resolve the noted discrepancies in the reported balances of the Property, Plant and Equipment accounts, and thereafter, require the periodic reconciliation of the balances of these accounts.
observations.reason: Discrepancy of ₱3.68 million between property and accounting records was noted in CY 2016; unserviceable properties items which remained undisposed increased to ₱10.53 million. The audit observation was restated in Part II of the Report.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Accountant should identify the causes and differences of the unreconciled reciprocal accounts, and thereafter cause the recording of the necessary adjustments.
observations.reason: The unreconciled balance of the reciprocal accounts totaled to ₱4,375,538.41 in 2012 CY 2016, or an increase of ₱336,506.66 from that of the preceding year.

observations.recommendation: The Fund Utilization Report for the transfer in the amount of ₱685,000.00 must be submitted including other documentary evidences as required by the aforementioned circular. In addition, the Fund Utilization Reports for the previous releases to CNDR should be submitted before another release for the LGU counterpart will be allowed; the LGU strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2007-001 in the granting, utilization and accounting of the funds released to CNDR to avoid suspension/disallowance of the transaction.
observations.reason: The Fund Utilization Report was not submitted including other documentary evidences as required by the aforementioned circular. A Notice of Suspension which matured into Notice of Disallowance was issued for this audit observation.

observations.recommendation: The Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer shall prepare a separate LDRRMFIP using the format prescribed in COA Cir. No. 2012-002 to determine the activities to be charged against the LDRRMF and Special Trust Fund.
observations.reason: The Report was not submitted despite repeated requests.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management immediately reconcile the General
Ledger and the Subsidiary Ledger balances in order to account the difference of
₱6,199,118.18 and maintain Subsidiary Ledger for General Fund.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to
identify the causes and differences of the unreconciled reciprocal accounts, and
thereafter cause the recording of the necessary adjustments.

We recommended that Management instruct the OIC-Municipal
Treasurer/Acting General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant to resolve
the noted discrepancies in the reported balances of the Property, Plant and
Equipment accounts, and thereafter, require the periodic reconciliation of the
balances of these accounts.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to
immediately reconcile the noted discrepancies in the balances of the GL and bank
statements under the Cash in Bank–LCCA account as of December 31, 2016 by
preparing the required Bank Reconciliation Statements and to take up the
necessary adjustments in the books.

We reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendations that
Management:
(a.) require the concerned officers and employees to immediately
settle/liquidate their outstanding cash advances thru the submission
of duly accomplished liquidation reports, otherwise withhold any
money due them;
(b.) stop the grant of additional cash advances for travel to all personnel
with unliquidated cash advance until such time these are fully settled;
and
(c.) monitor and establish controls to ensure the timely submission of
liquidation documents and full accounting of balances of cash
advance by enforcing strictly the prescribed period of liquidation.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to
record disallowances totaling ₱714,955.40 per NFDs issued by COA to avoid
understatement of the Other Receivables – Disallowances and Charges (1-03-06-
010) account.

We recommended that the Local School Board strictly comply with Section
272 of RA No. 7160 and Item 4.0 of the DECS, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No.
01, series of 1998 in its budget preparation and execution in order that programs,
projects and activities of public elementary and secondary schools are ultimately
prioritized.

We recommended that Management (a) require the recipients to issue AF 51
requisitioned from the Department of Education, Division of Camarines Sur to
acknowledge the receipt of financial assistance from the LGU and (b) the immediate
accounting/liquidation of the funds received, strictly observing the revised
guidelines and documentary requirements for common government transactions
prescribed under COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 to avoid audit
suspensions or disallowances.

We recommended that Management:
(a) evaluate the significant delays in the procurement process in
coordination with the concerned Offices and institute improvements
in the soonest possible time to achieve efficient delivery of goods and
services; and
(b) instruct the GSO to re-visit the procurement rules, taking into
consideration the timelines set by RA No. 9184.

We recommended that Management instruct all concerned municipal
officials to monitor the implementation of projects funded from the 20 per cent
Development Fund to attain the optimal socio-economic development and
environmental management outcomes which are the core of the fund allocation.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately reconcile the noted discrepancies in the balances of the GL and bank statements under the Cash in Bank – LCCA account as of December 31, 2015 by preparing the required Bank Reconciliation Statements and to take up the necessary adjustments in the books.
observations.reason: Incomplete submission of Bank Reconciliation Statements.

observations.recommendation: 1. Strictly adhere to the provision of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 and Section 89 of PD 1445 on the grant, utilization and settlement of cash advances; 2. Fully liquidate/collect all long outstanding cash advances in pursuance to existing rules and regulations; 3. Prepare demand letters for immediate settlements thereof; 4. Require all officers and employees to liquidate travel advances strictly within 30 days after return to official station, otherwise, suspend salary until liquidation has been done. Refund of unused cash advance should be made immediately and simultaneously with the liquidation. Within 10 days before expiration of the 30 day period, the Accountant must sent written reminder enjoining the official or employee concerned to liquidate his travel advances, in compliance with COA Circular No. 96-004 dated April 19, 1996.
observations.reason: Laxity in the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances. Restated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: Require the immediate refund of the total amount of ₱4,953 million according to individual liabilities of concerned municipal officials/employees to forestall the issuance of Notice of Disallowance.

Management immediately stop the practice of charging the wages of unrelated casual employees against the Market Operations Fund such that the appropriations for them will be included to the appropriations for the offices where they are assigned.

Management admonish the members of the Bids and Awards Committee to exercise diligence in the evaluation of the submitted documents establishing the qualifications of prospective bidders, and to consider the penalties that may be imposed upon them for violation of pertinent provisions of the amended Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of Republic Act No. 9184.
Management exercise due prudence in disbursing LGU funds for similar activities in the future.
Amend the 2002 Revenue Code and observe the provisions of Section 39 of the Local Treasury Operations manual.

The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal treasurer submit the required documents to establish the validity, reliability and propriety of claims/disbursements. Non-submission of the required documents will be grounds for suspension or disallowance of the transaction.

Municipality strictly adhere with the provisions of DILG & DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011. Programs and projects must be specifically identified and its purpose must be limited to Social and Economic Development and Environmental Management of the municipality as itemized/provided by the DILG & DBM Circular mentioned.

Strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 and with Section 6.9 of COA Circular No. 2009-2 dated May 18, 2009. Purchase Orders and contracts must be submitted five (5) days from the issuance thereof to avoid suspensions in the transactions involved and the penalty prescribed by law. Likewise, copies of Notices of Deliveries/Inspection and Acceptance Reports must be submitted to the Auditor within twenty-four (24) hours from acceptance of deliveries of goods.
observations.reason: Not all Purchase Orders and Contracts were submitted within the reglementary period.

observations.recommendation: Municipal Accountant submit the mandatory financial reports and accounts together with the supporting documents promptly and regularly within the reglementary period in compliance with the above-cited COA regulations.
observations.reason: Some financial reports were not submitted promptly/ submitted within the regularity period.

observations.recommendation: The Management prepare and submit a GAD Plan addressing the gender issues in accordance with the guidelines provided in the above cited Joint Circular. The GAD Plan prepared and formulated by the Local Development Council Resolution should follow the regular planning and budget schedule of the agency and submitted to the Sanggunian concerned for approval and adoption. Likewise, the yearly Accomplishment Report on GAD should be prepared and submitted to assess the implementation of the GAD Plan and Budget and to evaluate the effectiveness of GAD Programs, projects and Activities in addressing GAD concerns.
observations.reason: The Report was not submitted despite repeated requests.

observations.recommendation: Management shall strictly observe the above provision and jurisprudence in the disbursement of SEF.
observations.reason: Unrelated expenses are still being charged against the Fund. The audit observation is restated in Part II of the Report.

observations.recommendation: The Management shall effect the continuous implementation of Management practices. Start the process and implement appropriate steps that would lead to the closure of the controlled dumps and would result to switching to sanitary landfill. Intensify its effort to achieve and embrace the full concept of ESWM and adhere to the provision of RA 9003. Stop collecting non-segregated waste and come up with measure that would help its cause.
observations.reason: Implementation of activities related to the compliance with the Solid Waste Management Act are on-going.

observations.recommendation: Management direct the BAC to ensure that the bid security particularly the Bids Securing Declarations submitted by the bidders are compliant with the prescribed standard format and be notarized, otherwise the erring bidders be disqualified from participating in the public bidding.
